entries.title: Vanguard Action Squad Has Capture Not Massacre As Core Objective

context.label: Vanguard Action Squad attacks the camp

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Vanguard Action Squad’s key objective is to capture Bakugo, with violence and chaos used to isolate targets and overload heroes.
content.injection: During the camp attack, keep the villains’ main objective focused on capturing Bakugo rather than indiscriminate slaughter alone.
constraints: Individual villains may still endanger or attempt to kill students.
antiLore: Do not make the attack purposeless chaos with no capture objective.

entries.title: Mandalay Warning Turns The Attack Into Survival Mode

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mandalay’s telepathic warning shifts the camp from training to emergency survival and gives students information under pressure.
content.injection: After Mandalay’s warning, write students as reacting to a confirmed villain attack: scattered, frightened, and improvising under emergency instructions.
constraints: Not every student has a clear battlefield picture.
antiLore: Do not have all students instantly coordinate like a professional raid team.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Muscular Is A Direct Lethal Threat To Kota And Izuku

context.label: Izuku fights Muscular to protect Kota

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Muscular is a lethal villain whose confrontation with Kota forces Izuku into a desperate protective fight beyond his safe limits.
content.injection: In the Muscular fight, keep Izuku’s victory desperate, damaging, and protective; he wins to save Kota, not because he has stable high-output mastery.
constraints: Izuku’s body should be severely strained by the fight.
antiLore: Do not treat “one million percent” as a stable literal percentage or repeatable form.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: One For All
ability.stage: desperate burst beyond safe early Full Cowling
constraints: Izuku cannot safely reproduce this output as normal technique.
antiLore: Do not portray this as controlled one-million-percent mastery.

entries.title: Kota Changes Because Izuku Protects Him At Cost

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kota’s attitude shifts after Izuku protects him from Muscular at extreme personal cost.
content.injection: After Izuku saves Kota, Kota can begin reassessing heroes through Izuku specifically, but he must not instantly become a generic cheerful hero fan.
constraints: His change is personal and trauma-shaped.
antiLore: Do not erase Kota’s grief immediately after the rescue.

entries.title: Dark Shadow Becomes A Nighttime Liability

context.label: Moonfish threat triggers Dark Shadow crisis

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Tokoyami’s Dark Shadow becomes dangerously hard to control in darkness and crisis, turning a strength into a liability.
content.injection: During the camp attack at night, treat Dark Shadow as powerful but unstable; allies must manage the danger instead of assuming Tokoyami has clean control.
antiLore: Do not portray Tokoyami as fully mastering Dark Shadow during this arc.

entries.title: Shoji Acts As A Calm Protective Scout

context.label: Moonfish threat triggers Dark Shadow crisis

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoji’s calm, protective behavior and sensory utility help students survive the forest chaos and handle Tokoyami’s crisis.
content.injection: Use Shoji as a steady protector and scout during the camp attack, especially when sensory awareness or shielding classmates matters.
constraints: His usefulness is practical and tactical rather than flashy.
antiLore: Do not ignore Shoji when Tokoyami’s rampage or forest navigation is relevant.

entries.title: Mustard Uses Gas To Turn Terrain Against Students

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mustard’s gas creates a terrain-denial crisis that rewards endurance, observation, and cooperation rather than straightforward brawling.
content.injection: In the Mustard fight, make the gas cloud a tactical hazard that limits movement, vision, and breathing; Kendo and Tetsutetsu should respond through teamwork and durability.
constraints: Unprotected students must not casually breathe or ignore the gas.
antiLore: Do not reduce Mustard to a harmless child villain.

entries.title: Toga Samples Blood Under Cover Of Chaos

context.label: Toga steals blood during the camp attack

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Toga steals blood during the camp attack, creating a future disguise threat while most characters do not understand the full implications yet.
content.injection: After Toga samples blood, preserve the hidden future risk without making uninformed characters know exactly how her Transform Quirk will be used later.
constraints: The blood sample matters later but is not fully understood by most students here.
antiLore: Do not reveal later infiltration uses before the story reaches them.

entries.title: Mr Compress Converts The Capture Mission Into Misdirection

context.label: Mr. Compress captures Bakugo and Tokoyami

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mr. Compress uses his Quirk and showman misdirection to compress and transport Bakugo and Tokoyami, turning the attack into a capture crisis.
content.injection: When Compress acts at the camp, make his threat tactical and deceptive: he can steal targets in compressed form and force students to identify the real marbles under pressure.
constraints: His Quirk is not brute force; it is capture, misdirection, and escape utility.
antiLore: Do not portray Bakugo’s capture as simple overpowering without Compress’s trickery.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: Compress
constraints: Targets can be compressed and concealed as marbles.
antiLore: Do not make Compress a generic brawler.

entries.title: Tokoyami Recovery Does Not Undo Bakugo Capture

context.label: Tokoyami recovered while Bakugo remains taken

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Tokoyami is recovered from Compress’s capture, but Bakugo remains taken by the League.
content.injection: After the marble scramble, keep the outcome precise: Tokoyami is recovered, while Bakugo is successfully captured and removed from U.A. protection.
constraints: Students and teachers should experience this as a failure despite avoiding a worse outcome.
antiLore: Do not leave Tokoyami captured into the Hideout Raid arc.

entries.title: Bakugo Capture Is A Strategic And Public Disaster

context.label: Bakugo is taken by the League of Villains

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo's capture is both a rescue crisis and a public proof that U.A. failed to protect students.
content.injection: After Bakugo is taken, pressure U.A. legally, emotionally, and publicly; this is not just a missing-person subplot.
constraints: The public consequences matter as much as the immediate rescue concern.
antiLore: Do not treat Bakugo’s kidnapping as a minor field-trip complication.

entries.title: Aoyama Camp Behavior Should Stay Ambiguous Not Fully Revealed

context.label: Bakugo is taken by the League of Villains

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aoyama’s behavior at the camp can seem strange or fearful, but the later U.A. traitor reveal must not be exposed in this arc.
content.injection: If Aoyama appears during the camp crisis, keep him frightened or evasive without revealing later traitor context.
constraints: Readers may notice odd behavior; characters must not know the later answer.
antiLore: Do not state or imply the U.A. traitor reveal before its proper arc.

content.injection: During the attack, treat Bakugo and Todoroki as especially visible student assets; protecting them has tactical and symbolic importance.

entries.title: Camp Arc Should Not Reveal Bakugo Future Growth

context.label: Bakugo is taken by the League of Villains

coordinates.label: Deku early Full Cowling with catastrophic burst risk

content.fact: Bakugo’s later emotional growth and relationship repair should remain future canon during the camp and Kamino crisis.
content.injection: Keep Bakugo in his pre-rematch emotional state here: proud, defiant, and guarded, without later clarity about Izuku and All Might.
